name: pages-router
context: Build Next.js apps with the Pages Router — getStaticProps, getServerSideProps, _app.tsx, and classic file-based routing
info: The Pages Router is Next.js's original routing system using the `pages/` directory. Each file in `pages/` becomes a route: `pages/index.tsx` maps to `/`, `pages/about.tsx` maps to `/about`. Data fetching uses exported functions: `getStaticProps` for static generation at build time, `getServerSideProps` for server-side rendering per request, and `getStaticPaths` for dynamic routes with static generation. Custom `_app.tsx` wraps all pages with shared layout and state, `_document.tsx` customizes the HTML document structure. API routes live in `pages/api/`. The Pages Router is stable and widely deployed but no longer receives new features — the App Router is recommended for new projects. Agents encounter Pages Router in existing codebases that haven't migrated.
triggers: add a page with pages router
migrate from pages to app router

sections.title: getStaticProps (Static Generation)
sections.body: Export `getStaticProps` from a page to fetch data at build time. Returns `{ props }` passed to the page component. Add `revalidate: seconds` for Incremental Static Regeneration (ISR). The function runs only on the server — code here is never bundled to the client. Use for content that can be pre-rendered: blog posts, product pages, marketing pages.

sections.title: getServerSideProps (Server-Side Rendering)
sections.body: Export `getServerSideProps` from a page to fetch data on every request. Receives `context` with `req`, `res`, `params`, `query`, and `resolvedUrl`. Returns `{ props }`, `{ redirect }`, or `{ notFound: true }`. Use for pages that need request-time data: user dashboards, authenticated pages, real-time data. Slower than static generation since it runs per-request.

sections.title: getStaticPaths (Dynamic Static Routes)
sections.body: Export `getStaticPaths` alongside `getStaticProps` for dynamic routes. Returns `{ paths, fallback }`. `paths` is an array of `{ params }` objects defining which routes to pre-render. `fallback: false` returns 404 for unknown paths, `fallback: true` renders a loading state then generates the page, `fallback: 'blocking'` server-renders on first request then caches.

sections.title: Custom App and Document
sections.body: `_app.tsx` wraps every page — use for global layout, state providers, CSS imports, and error boundaries. `_document.tsx` controls the HTML shell — use for custom `<html>` attributes, `<head>` elements, and `<body>` attributes. `_document.tsx` only renders on the server. Both files are optional but common in production apps.

links.context: Data fetching patterns including Pages Router methods

related.note: App Router is the successor to Pages Router with Server Components and streaming
getStaticProps and getServerSideProps are Pages Router data fetching methods
API routes in pages/api/ are part of the Pages Router
